conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first check the official eligibility criteria so I can guide you accurately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out! I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To get started, I need to ask you a few questions. First:

**Do you have any children for whom you're inquiring about Child Benefit?** If yes, could you please tell me how many children you have and their names?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. Her name is Alex and she's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is she enrolled in A-levels, higher education, or an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not in any education or training at the moment. She finished school and isn't studying anything right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I need to ask a couple more questions to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does she work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she works full-time at a local shop.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just to confirm:

**Does Alex work 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she does. She works full-time, so it's definitely more than 24 hours a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also confirm one final detail:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: While Child Benefit can continue for children up to age 20 if they remain in approved education or training, Alex is 18 and is no longer in any education or training. Additionally, she is working full-time at more than 24 hours per week.

According to the official rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week **and** is no longer in approved education or training. Since both of these conditions apply to Alex, your eligibility for Child Benefit has ended.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future (for example, if she decides to go back into approved education or training and reduces her work hours), you may want to contact the Child Benefit Office to discuss your situation again.

Is there anything else I can help clarify about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. However, Alex has left school and is not currently studying or in any approved training. Additionally, Alex is working full-time (more than 24 hours per week). The rules specifically state that you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since both conditions apply to Alex (she works 24+ hours per week AND is not in approved education or training), the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex has finished school and is not in any approved education or training. She is working full-time (more than 24 hours per week) at a local shop. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility stops when a child aged 16 or over works 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. However, Alex has left school and is not currently studying or in any approved training. Additionally, Alex is working full-time (more than 24 hours per week). The rules specifically state that you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since both conditions apply to Alex (she works 24+ hours per week AND is not in approved education or training), the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex has finished school and is not in any approved education or training. She is working full-time (more than 24 hours per week) at a local shop. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility stops when a child aged 16 or over works 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
